title: Online Play via Wiimmfi
requirements: An SD card, FAT32 formatted (for the on-console patcher app)
An internet connection on the Wii
warnings: Nintendo shut down the WFC (Nintendo Wi-Fi Connection) servers in 2014. **Wiimmfi** is a community-run replacement, not an official Nintendo service.
The DNS method affects every game and channel that tries to go online, not just the one you want patched — some unsupported games may show connection errors as a result.
_note: Wii mini has no Wi-Fi hardware at all, so online play (real or via Wiimmfi) isn't possible on it. This guide applies to standard Wii and Wii Family Edition only.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**Wiimmfi** restores online multiplayer for Wii games after Nintendo shut down WFC in 2014. There are three ways to use it:

1. **DNS method** — point your Wii (or router) at Wiimmfi's DNS server. Works automatically for supported disc games, no SD card needed.
2. **Priiloader method** — a System Menu Hack that patches games automatically when launched from the Disc Channel. Needs Priiloader 0.9+.
3. **ISO Patcher method** — for game backups run through a loader. You patch the ISO on a PC before playing.

This guide covers the DNS method (easiest) and the on-console **Wiimmfi Patcher** app used with the ISO method. Pick whichever fits how you play.

steps.title: Method 1: Change your Wii's DNS
steps.body:
From the Wii Menu, go to **Wii Options** → **Wii Settings** → **Page 2** → **Internet** → **Connection Settings**.

Select your connection slot and choose **Change Settings**.

Set **Auto-Obtain DNS** to **No**, then open **Advanced Settings**.

Set:
- **Primary DNS**: `95.217.77.181`
- **Secondary DNS**: `1.1.1.1`

Click **Confirm**, then **Save**, then run the connection test.

Once connected, supported disc games (Mario Kart Wii and many others) connect straight to Wiimmfi through the normal online menu — no further setup needed.

steps.title: Method 2: Priiloader auto-patch
steps.body:
If you'd rather not change your DNS globally, Priiloader can patch games automatically when you launch them from the Disc Channel.

Requires **Priiloader 0.9 or later** on **System Menu 4.3**, with the latest `hacks_hash.ini`.

1. Boot into Priiloader (hold **RESET** while powering on).
2. Go to **System Menu Hacks**.
3. Enable **Wiimmfi patch v4** — if it shows a different version number, your `hacks_hash.ini` is out of date; update Priiloader's hack list first.
4. Save settings and boot to System Menu.

Now launching a supported game from the Disc Channel patches it on the fly to connect to Wiimmfi.

steps.title: Method 3: Put the on-console patcher on your SD card
steps.body:
If you play from **backups/ISOs** through a USB loader instead of discs, you need to patch the image itself. There are two ways:

- **On a PC**: use the Wiimmfi ISO Patcher from wiimmfi.de/patcher (a Windows/PC tool — download it there and point it at your ISO/WBFS/CISO/WIA/WDF/GCZ file; NKIT files aren't supported).
- **On the console**: Graphite can put the **Wiimmfi Patcher** homebrew app on your SD card, which patches games already sitting on your loader's storage device.

This step installs the on-console app.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull it out.

steps.title: Run the Wiimmfi Patcher
steps.body:
Put the SD card back in your Wii, launch the **Homebrew Channel**, and open **Wiimmfi Patcher**.

Select your game backup from the list and confirm to patch it. The app rewrites the game's online connection info to point at Wiimmfi instead of the dead Nintendo servers.

Launch the patched game through your loader as usual — online play should now go through Wiimmfi.

troubleshoot.label: The game still won't connect online

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Whichever method you used, supported games should now connect to Wiimmfi for online play instead of failing silently against Nintendo's dead servers.

Check wiimmfi.de for the current list of supported games before assuming a title just doesn't work.
